characters.name: Richard Hannay
characters.description: A thirty-seven-year-old man from South Africa who has returned to England after making his fortune, but finds himself bored and disillusioned with life in London.

characters.name: Franklin P. Scudder
characters.description: An American from Kentucky who worked as a war correspondent and became involved in uncovering a conspiracy, now in hiding and presumed dead.

physical.appearance: slim man, with a short brown beard and small, gimlety blue eyes

characters.name: Constantine Karolides
characters.description: The Greek Premier who is described as the one big honest man in European politics and is targeted for assassination.

characters.name: Paddock
characters.description: Hannay's servant, a loyal man whom Hannay helped in Africa and brought to England as his valet.

characters.name: Julia Czechenyi
characters.description: A woman mentioned by Scudder as being involved in the conspiracy against Karolides, intended to serve as a decoy.

timeline.event: Hannay returns from the City feeling disgusted with life in England
Hannay reads about Karolides in the evening papers at his club
Hannay encounters Scudder outside his flat and lets him in
Scudder tells Hannay about the conspiracy and his fake death
Scudder disguises himself as Captain Digby
Hannay learns about the 'suicide' in No. 15
Scudder stays hidden in Hannay's flat for several days
Hannay discovers Scudder murdered in his flat
timeline.date_in_story: about half-past ten at night, several days later

settings.name: London
settings.description: The city where Hannay lives, described as busy with crowds on pavements, shop-girls, clerks, and dandies. Includes areas like Portland Place, Oxford Circus, and Langham Place.

settings.name: Hannay's club
settings.description: A rather pot-house club that takes in Colonial members, where Hannay goes to drink and read evening papers.

settings.name: Café Royal
settings.description: Where Hannay dines before going to a music-hall.

settings.name: Hannay's flat
settings.description: First floor in a new block behind Langham Place, with a common staircase, porter and liftman at entrance, each flat shut off from others. Has a back room for smoking and writing letters, and a smoking-room.

settings.name: No. 15
settings.description: The flat where Scudder staged his fake death, on the top floor of the same building as Hannay.

world_rules: There is an international conspiracy involving anarchists and financiers working to destabilize Europe
The conspiracy aims to set Russia and Germany against each other
Political assassinations are orchestrated to look like they come from rival nations to create international incidents
Intelligence networks operate across European cities, tracking individuals through multiple countries
British Foreign Office hosts international diplomatic gatherings that serve as opportunities for political operations

voice_profile.style_notes: Conversational narrative style with British colonial perspective, building tension through mundane details before revealing dramatic events, uses period-appropriate language and expressions

plot_threads.thread: Who killed Scudder and will they come after Hannay next?
Will the conspiracy to assassinate Karolides on June 15th succeed?
What role will Hannay play in stopping the conspiracy now that Scudder is dead?
Who are the mysterious figures behind the conspiracy, including the Black Stone and the man who lisps?
How will Hannay deal with having a murdered man in his flat?
